feed.title: Control and Automation for Intelligent Systems Lab
feed.subtitle: Research in the Control and Automation for Intelligent Systems Lab focuses on developing, analyzing, and applying control theory and artificial intelligence to improve the safety, flexibility, and performance of complex, dynamic systems, such as manufacturing systems.

entry.title: ICDS Funds Mid-Scale Seed Grants
entry.content:
Dr. Kovalenko has received support through the Penn State Institute for Computational and Data Sciences (ICDS) Mid-Scale Seed Grant Program.

The project “Water Evaluation and Treatment Digital Twin (WET-DT): A Framework for Robust, Predictive Process Controls for Water Treatment Plants,” was selected as part of a new ICDS initiative supporting interdisciplinary research across the University. The project brings together collaborators from University Park and Penn State Behrend and contributes to ICDS research in digital twins and computational/data-driven science.

Read more at the Penn State Institute for Computational and Data Sciences (ICDS).
entry.summary: Dr. Kovalenko has received support through the Penn State Institute for Computational and Data Sciences (ICDS) Mid-Scale Seed Grant Program.

entry.title: Welcome New Undergraduate Researcher
entry.content: Welcome to Elijah Gruver and Chaitanya Patel, who joined the lab as an undergraduate researcher!
entry.summary: Welcome to Elijah Gruver and Chaitanya Patel, who joined the lab as an undergraduate researcher!

entry.title: Paper Accepted to ACC 2026
entry.content: The paper “Energy-Aware Integrated Predictive Maintenance Planning and Production Scheduling” has been accepted to the 2026 American Control Conference (ACC).
entry.summary: The paper “Energy-Aware Integrated Predictive Maintenance Planning and Production Scheduling” has been accepted to the 2026 American Control Conference (ACC).

entry.title: Paper Accepted to Robotics and Computer-Integrated Manufacturing

link.title: Paper Accepted to Robotics and Computer-Integrated Manufacturing

entry.content: The paper titled “Adaptive task planning and coordination in multi-agent manufacturing systems using large language models” has been accepted for publication in Robotics and Computer-Integrated Manufacturing.
entry.summary: The paper titled “Adaptive task planning and coordination in multi-agent manufacturing systems using large language models” has been accepted for publication in Robotics and Computer-Integrated Manufacturing.

entry.title: White Elephant and Holiday Party
entry.content: The CAIS lab enjoys a White Elephant gift exchange with Dr. Fitzsimons’s, Dr. Meira-Goes’s and Dr. Zhong’s labs!
entry.summary: The CAIS lab enjoys a White Elephant gift exchange with Dr. Fitzsimons’s, Dr. Meira-Goes’s and Dr. Zhong’s labs!

entry.title: Paper Accepted to IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering

link.title: Paper Accepted to IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering

entry.content: The paper titled “Hierarchical Model Predictive Control for Energy-Aware Scheduling of Digital Twin-Based Batch Manufacturing Systems” has been accepted for publication in IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering.
entry.summary: The paper titled “Hierarchical Model Predictive Control for Energy-Aware Scheduling of Digital Twin-Based Batch Manufacturing Systems” has been accepted for publication in IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering.

entry.title: End-of-Semester Potluck
entry.content: The CAIS lab enjoys a potluck party to celebrate the end of the semester!
entry.summary: The CAIS lab enjoys a potluck party to celebrate the end of the semester!

entry.title: Modeling Estimation and Control Conference (MECC 2025)
entry.content: Ilya, Hongliang, Adelaide, and Evan attended the Modeling Estimation and Control Conference (MECC 2024) in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania. Hongliang’s work was nominated for a best student paper award and he was selected as a “Rising Star” in the ASME Dynamic Systems and Control Division. Adelaide presented a poster and a short talk on her summer work for modeling a water treatment testbed. Ilya provided an overview of his recent CAREER award.
entry.summary: Ilya, Hongliang, Adelaide, and Evan attended the Modeling Estimation and Control Conference (MECC 2024) in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania. Hongliang’s work was nominated for a best student paper award and he was selected as a “Rising Star” in the ASME Dynamic Systems and Control Division. Adelaide presented a poster and a short talk on her summer work for modeling a water treatment testbed. Ilya provided an overview of his recent CAREER award.

entry.title: Welcome New Undergraduate Researcher
entry.content: Welcome to Huihang Liu, who joined the lab as an undergraduate researcher!
entry.summary: Welcome to Huihang Liu, who joined the lab as an undergraduate researcher!

entry.title: IEEE International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering (CASE 2025)

link.title: IEEE International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering (CASE 2025)

entry.content:
Hongliang, Jonghan, Yifei and Greg presented their research at the IEEE International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering (CASE 2025) in Los Angeles, USA.

Ilya co-organized the 1st Workshop on Robust Machine Intelligence for Factory Automation at the conference.
entry.summary: Hongliang, Jonghan, Yifei and Greg presented their research at the IEEE International Conference on Automation Science and Engineering (CASE 2025) in Los Angeles, USA.
